title: regime_sample_dag
description: Per-sample directed-graph summary of regime membership across the windows overlapping a candidate interval. Produced by lib_recomb_combination.R::derive_R_from_regime (DAG formulation, chat-12). For each sample, the per-window regime labels from C01j regime_memberships are collapsed by rle(); each distinct run becomes a node with a window-count weight, and each transition between consecutive runs becomes an edge. The deviation_fraction scalar (1 − dominant_run_windows / total_windows) is the primary per-sample recombinant-signal statistic: a sample whose interval is mostly one regime but contains a non-trivial stretch of a different regime has a non-zero deviation_fraction, and when that stretch is long enough in bp it fires R. Cohort-level aggregates feed C01d Layer C (inversion-vs-family / complex-system scoring) via keys_extracted.

per_sample.description: One row per sample with at least one window overlapping the interval. Samples with zero overlap windows are NOT included (they are uninformative, not 'no deviation').

n_windows.description: Total windows for this sample inside the interval.

n_distinct_nodes.description: Number of distinct regime labels visited (= number of unique rle values).

n_edges.description: Number of transitions between consecutive runs (= length(rle$values) - 1, 0 if only one run).

dominant_regime.description: Regime label with the most windows (argmax over summed run lengths, ties broken by first occurrence).

dominant_run_windows.description: Total windows assigned to the dominant regime (summed across possibly-multiple runs of that regime).

deviation_fraction.description: 1 − dominant_run_windows / n_windows. Key scalar: fraction of the sample's interval not in the dominant regime.

longest_deviation_windows.description: Length in windows of the longest contiguous non-dominant run. 0 if the sample never leaves its dominant regime.

longest_deviation_bp.description: longest_deviation_windows mapped to bp via the window-bp span. NA in practice is encoded as 0 here to keep the type strict.

terminal_matches_start.description: TRUE iff the first run's regime label equals the last run's regime label. A noise-like deviation tends to return to the dominant; a true recombinant often does NOT — so FALSE is weak additional recombinant evidence.

R_fired.description: Gate for the combination rule. Fires when deviation_fraction ≥ params.min_deviation_fraction AND longest_deviation_bp ≥ params.min_deviation_bp.

dag_nodes_compact.description: Optional compact textual rendering of the DAG as regime labels joined by '→', e.g. 'A→B→A'. Diagnostic aid for the per-sample DAG plot.

dag_node_weights.description: Window counts per run in the same order as dag_nodes_compact.

n_samples_with_deviation.description: Samples with deviation_fraction > 0 (any non-dominant window at all).

n_samples_R_fired.description: Samples where R_fired is TRUE — passed both the fraction and bp gates.

dominant_regime_cohort.description: Mode of per-sample dominant regimes. 'This interval is basically <X>' at the cohort level.

dominant_regime_support.description: Fraction of samples whose dominant regime equals dominant_regime_cohort. High value ⇒ coherent single-regime interval.

params.description: Parameters used for the DAG derivation, recorded for reproducibility.

window_size_bp.description: Median bp span of one window in the regime track; used for the windows→bp mapping of longest_deviation.
